Package com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components

Class CalculateCashBalanceResponse

java.lang.Object
com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components.CalculateCashBalanceResponse
public class CalculateCashBalanceResponse extends Object
CalculateCashBalanceResponse - The cash balance of an account.

  • Constructor Details

    • CalculateCashBalanceResponse

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse(Optional<String> account, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AvailableCashToWithdrawAmount> availableCashToWithdrawAmount, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AvailableLiquidityAmount> availableLiquidityAmount, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends FullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount> fullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount, Optional<Boolean> includeFullyPaidUnsettledFunds, Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTradeSummary>> intradayBuyTrades, Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> intradayDeposits, Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> intradayReturns, Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> intradayWithdrawals, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OpenBalanceAmount> openBalanceAmount, Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseOpenOrderSummary>> openBuyOrders, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OpenLiquidityAmount> openLiquidityAmount, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends PendingDebitDividendsAmount> pendingDebitDividendsAmount, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends PendingDebitInterestAmount> pendingDebitInterestAmount, Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> pendingWithdrawals, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends UnadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount> unadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends WithheldDepositThresholdAmount> withheldDepositThresholdAmount, Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> withheldDeposits)

    • CalculateCashBalanceResponse

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse()

  • Method Details

    • account

      public Optional<String> account()
      The account.

    • availableCashToWithdrawAmount

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<AvailableCashToWithdrawAmount> availableCashToWithdrawAmount()
      The account's available cash to withdraw in USD. It is calculated based on the `open_balance_amount` and account activity. This value is always non-negative. If `unadjusted_available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` is negative, this value will be `0`.

    • availableLiquidityAmount

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<AvailableLiquidityAmount> availableLiquidityAmount()
      The account's available liquidity in USD including the value of cash and equity positions. It is calculated based on the `open_liquidity_amount` and account activity. This value can be negative.

    • fullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<FullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount> fullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount()
      The account's fully paid unsettled funds in USD. Fully paid unsettled funds are proceeds from sales of assets paid for with cash and not bought on margin. If `include_fully_paid_unsettled_funds` is `false`, this value is **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • includeFullyPaidUnsettledFunds

      public Optional<Boolean> includeFullyPaidUnsettledFunds()
      Whether fully paid unsettled funds are included in balances.

    • intradayBuyTrades

      public Optional<List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTradeSummary>> intradayBuyTrades()
      The account's intraday buy trades. The net amounts are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since trades have positive amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • intradayDeposits

      public Optional<List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> intradayDeposits()
      The account's eligible intraday deposits that have posted to the ledger. Intraday deposits may be eligible based on mechanism (e.g. intraday ICT deposits are eligible for ICT withdrawals, but not ACH withdrawals). The amounts are **added** to `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since deposits have positive amounts, the calculated values will **increase**.

    • intradayReturns

      public Optional<List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> intradayReturns()
      The account's intraday returns. The amounts are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Returned deposits will **decrease** calculated values because they have positive amounts and returned withdrawals will **increase** calculated values because they have negative amounts.

    • intradayWithdrawals

      public Optional<List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> intradayWithdrawals()
      The account's intraday withdrawals that have posted to the ledger. The amounts are **added** to `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since withdrawals have negative amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • openBalanceAmount

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OpenBalanceAmount> openBalanceAmount()
      The account's starting cash balance for the day in USD as calculated by the margin system. It is used as the base to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • openBuyOrders

      public Optional<List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseOpenOrderSummary>> openBuyOrders()
      The account's open buy orders. The expected notional ceilings are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since open orders have positive expected notional ceilings, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • openLiquidityAmount

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OpenLiquidityAmount> openLiquidityAmount()
      The account's starting liquidity for the day in USD as calculated by the margin system. It includes the value of all asset positions (e.g. cash, equities, fixed income, etc). It is used as the base to calculate `available_liquidity_amount`. This value can be negative.

    • pendingDebitDividendsAmount

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<PendingDebitDividendsAmount> pendingDebitDividendsAmount()
      The account's pending debit dividends in USD. The value is **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • pendingDebitInterestAmount

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<PendingDebitInterestAmount> pendingDebitInterestAmount()
      The account's pending debit interest in USD. The value is **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • pendingWithdrawals

      public Optional<List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> pendingWithdrawals()
      The account's pending withdrawals. Pending withdrawals may need review and have yet to post to the ledger. The amounts are **added** to `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since withdrawals have negative amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • unadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<UnadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount> unadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount()
      The account's unadjusted available cash to withdraw in USD. It is calculated based on the `open_balance_amount` and account activity. This value can be negative.

    • withheldDepositThresholdAmount

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<WithheldDepositThresholdAmount> withheldDepositThresholdAmount()
      The account's withheld deposit threshold. Amounts in `withheld_deposits` up to this threshold will be not be subtracted to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • withheldDeposits

      public Optional<List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> withheldDeposits()
      The account's withheld deposits. Deposits are withheld if there is a risk of a return (e.g. ACH deposits). Typically, they are withheld for 5 business days after completing. The amounts are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since deposits have positive amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**. However, withheld deposits will not be subtracted up to the `withheld_deposit_threshold_amount`.

    • builder

      public static final CalculateCashBalanceResponse.Builder builder()

    • withAccount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withAccount(String account)
      The account.

    • withAccount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withAccount(Optional<String> account)
      The account.

    • withAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount(AvailableCashToWithdrawAmount availableCashToWithdrawAmount)
      The account's available cash to withdraw in USD. It is calculated based on the `open_balance_amount` and account activity. This value is always non-negative. If `unadjusted_available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` is negative, this value will be `0`.

    • withAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AvailableCashToWithdrawAmount> availableCashToWithdrawAmount)
      The account's available cash to withdraw in USD. It is calculated based on the `open_balance_amount` and account activity. This value is always non-negative. If `unadjusted_available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` is negative, this value will be `0`.

    • withAvailableLiquidityAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withAvailableLiquidityAmount(AvailableLiquidityAmount availableLiquidityAmount)
      The account's available liquidity in USD including the value of cash and equity positions. It is calculated based on the `open_liquidity_amount` and account activity. This value can be negative.

    • withAvailableLiquidityAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withAvailableLiquidityAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends AvailableLiquidityAmount> availableLiquidityAmount)
      The account's available liquidity in USD including the value of cash and equity positions. It is calculated based on the `open_liquidity_amount` and account activity. This value can be negative.

    • withFullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withFullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount(FullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount fullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount)
      The account's fully paid unsettled funds in USD. Fully paid unsettled funds are proceeds from sales of assets paid for with cash and not bought on margin. If `include_fully_paid_unsettled_funds` is `false`, this value is **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • withFullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withFullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends FullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount> fullyPaidUnsettledFundsAmount)
      The account's fully paid unsettled funds in USD. Fully paid unsettled funds are proceeds from sales of assets paid for with cash and not bought on margin. If `include_fully_paid_unsettled_funds` is `false`, this value is **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • withIncludeFullyPaidUnsettledFunds

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withIncludeFullyPaidUnsettledFunds(boolean includeFullyPaidUnsettledFunds)
      Whether fully paid unsettled funds are included in balances.

    • withIncludeFullyPaidUnsettledFunds

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withIncludeFullyPaidUnsettledFunds(Optional<Boolean> includeFullyPaidUnsettledFunds)
      Whether fully paid unsettled funds are included in balances.

    • withIntradayBuyTrades

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withIntradayBuyTrades(List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTradeSummary> intradayBuyTrades)
      The account's intraday buy trades. The net amounts are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since trades have positive amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • withIntradayBuyTrades

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withIntradayBuyTrades(Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTradeSummary>> intradayBuyTrades)
      The account's intraday buy trades. The net amounts are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since trades have positive amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • withIntradayDeposits

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withIntradayDeposits(List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary> intradayDeposits)
      The account's eligible intraday deposits that have posted to the ledger. Intraday deposits may be eligible based on mechanism (e.g. intraday ICT deposits are eligible for ICT withdrawals, but not ACH withdrawals). The amounts are **added** to `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since deposits have positive amounts, the calculated values will **increase**.

    • withIntradayDeposits

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withIntradayDeposits(Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> intradayDeposits)
      The account's eligible intraday deposits that have posted to the ledger. Intraday deposits may be eligible based on mechanism (e.g. intraday ICT deposits are eligible for ICT withdrawals, but not ACH withdrawals). The amounts are **added** to `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since deposits have positive amounts, the calculated values will **increase**.

    • withIntradayReturns

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withIntradayReturns(List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary> intradayReturns)
      The account's intraday returns. The amounts are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Returned deposits will **decrease** calculated values because they have positive amounts and returned withdrawals will **increase** calculated values because they have negative amounts.

    • withIntradayReturns

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withIntradayReturns(Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> intradayReturns)
      The account's intraday returns. The amounts are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Returned deposits will **decrease** calculated values because they have positive amounts and returned withdrawals will **increase** calculated values because they have negative amounts.

    • withIntradayWithdrawals

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withIntradayWithdrawals(List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary> intradayWithdrawals)
      The account's intraday withdrawals that have posted to the ledger. The amounts are **added** to `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since withdrawals have negative amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • withIntradayWithdrawals

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withIntradayWithdrawals(Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> intradayWithdrawals)
      The account's intraday withdrawals that have posted to the ledger. The amounts are **added** to `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since withdrawals have negative amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • withOpenBalanceAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withOpenBalanceAmount(OpenBalanceAmount openBalanceAmount)
      The account's starting cash balance for the day in USD as calculated by the margin system. It is used as the base to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • withOpenBalanceAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withOpenBalanceAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OpenBalanceAmount> openBalanceAmount)
      The account's starting cash balance for the day in USD as calculated by the margin system. It is used as the base to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • withOpenBuyOrders

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withOpenBuyOrders(List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseOpenOrderSummary> openBuyOrders)
      The account's open buy orders. The expected notional ceilings are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since open orders have positive expected notional ceilings, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • withOpenBuyOrders

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withOpenBuyOrders(Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseOpenOrderSummary>> openBuyOrders)
      The account's open buy orders. The expected notional ceilings are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since open orders have positive expected notional ceilings, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • withOpenLiquidityAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withOpenLiquidityAmount(OpenLiquidityAmount openLiquidityAmount)
      The account's starting liquidity for the day in USD as calculated by the margin system. It includes the value of all asset positions (e.g. cash, equities, fixed income, etc). It is used as the base to calculate `available_liquidity_amount`. This value can be negative.

    • withOpenLiquidityAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withOpenLiquidityAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends OpenLiquidityAmount> openLiquidityAmount)
      The account's starting liquidity for the day in USD as calculated by the margin system. It includes the value of all asset positions (e.g. cash, equities, fixed income, etc). It is used as the base to calculate `available_liquidity_amount`. This value can be negative.

    • withPendingDebitDividendsAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withPendingDebitDividendsAmount(PendingDebitDividendsAmount pendingDebitDividendsAmount)
      The account's pending debit dividends in USD. The value is **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • withPendingDebitDividendsAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withPendingDebitDividendsAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends PendingDebitDividendsAmount> pendingDebitDividendsAmount)
      The account's pending debit dividends in USD. The value is **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • withPendingDebitInterestAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withPendingDebitInterestAmount(PendingDebitInterestAmount pendingDebitInterestAmount)
      The account's pending debit interest in USD. The value is **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • withPendingDebitInterestAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withPendingDebitInterestAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends PendingDebitInterestAmount> pendingDebitInterestAmount)
      The account's pending debit interest in USD. The value is **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • withPendingWithdrawals

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withPendingWithdrawals(List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary> pendingWithdrawals)
      The account's pending withdrawals. Pending withdrawals may need review and have yet to post to the ledger. The amounts are **added** to `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since withdrawals have negative amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • withPendingWithdrawals

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withPendingWithdrawals(Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> pendingWithdrawals)
      The account's pending withdrawals. Pending withdrawals may need review and have yet to post to the ledger. The amounts are **added** to `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since withdrawals have negative amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**.

    • withUnadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withUnadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount(UnadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount unadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount)
      The account's unadjusted available cash to withdraw in USD. It is calculated based on the `open_balance_amount` and account activity. This value can be negative.

    • withUnadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withUnadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends UnadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount> unadjustedAvailableCashToWithdrawAmount)
      The account's unadjusted available cash to withdraw in USD. It is calculated based on the `open_balance_amount` and account activity. This value can be negative.

    • withWithheldDepositThresholdAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withWithheldDepositThresholdAmount(WithheldDepositThresholdAmount withheldDepositThresholdAmount)
      The account's withheld deposit threshold. Amounts in `withheld_deposits` up to this threshold will be not be subtracted to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • withWithheldDepositThresholdAmount

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withWithheldDepositThresholdAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends WithheldDepositThresholdAmount> withheldDepositThresholdAmount)
      The account's withheld deposit threshold. Amounts in `withheld_deposits` up to this threshold will be not be subtracted to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. This value is always non-negative.

    • withWithheldDeposits

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withWithheldDeposits(List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary> withheldDeposits)
      The account's withheld deposits. Deposits are withheld if there is a risk of a return (e.g. ACH deposits). Typically, they are withheld for 5 business days after completing. The amounts are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since deposits have positive amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**. However, withheld deposits will not be subtracted up to the `withheld_deposit_threshold_amount`.

    • withWithheldDeposits

      public CalculateCashBalanceResponse withWithheldDeposits(Optional<? extends List<CalculateCashBalanceResponseTransferSummary>> withheldDeposits)
      The account's withheld deposits. Deposits are withheld if there is a risk of a return (e.g. ACH deposits). Typically, they are withheld for 5 business days after completing. The amounts are **subtracted** from `open_balance_amount` and `open_liquidity_amount` to calculate `available_cash_to_withdraw_amount` and `available_liquidity_amount`. Since deposits have positive amounts, the calculated values will **decrease**. However, withheld deposits will not be subtracted up to the `withheld_deposit_threshold_amount`.

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object

      Overrides:
